title: Lab: Recover a Broken Animal-Support Directory
summary: Lab: Recover a Broken Animal-Support Directory uses an original animal support directory incident scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html elements nesting, html attributes without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real animal support directory incident artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

steps.title: directory user-journey acceptance test predict 1 · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Restore an animal-support directory whose image paths, link destinations, alternative text, and card headings fail for changed records and input methods. Which conclusion is supported by a changed animal record, missing-image behavior, meaningful link destinations, keyboard navigation, and a useful assistive outline? Work context: animal support directory incident.
steps.why: Lab: Recover a Broken Animal-Support Directory requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the animal support directory incident.

content.title: animal support directory incident · evidence decision
content.text: Commit before feedback, then connect the selected claim to the animal support directory incident evidence.

hints: Lab: Recover a Broken Animal-Support Directory: first isolate the evidence required by “directory user-journey acceptance test predict 1.”
Lab: Recover a Broken Animal-Support Directory: compare the current artifact with the named predict goal for step 1.
Lab: Recover a Broken Animal-Support Directory: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “directory user-journey acceptance test predict 1.”

options.text: Animal support directory incident case 1: indenting a paragraph beneath main makes it a child even if the tags close out of order. Accept the visible directory user-journey acceptance test result as proof.

Animal support directory incident case 1: parent-child relationships come from correctly nested tags; indentation should then mirror that structure. Apply it to the directory user-journey acceptance test changed case.

Animal support directory incident case 1: a browser screenshot proves the source tree is correctly nested. Skip a changed animal record, missing-image behavior, meaningful link destinations, keyboard navigation, and a useful assistive outline.

content.text: Reconstruct the animal support directory incident sequence by dependency, not by how familiar each term looks.

options.text: animal support directory incident phase 7.1: choose an element that represents the outer relationship
animal support directory incident phase 7.2: open the parent before its children
animal support directory incident phase 7.3: complete and close each child in order
animal support directory incident phase 7.4: close the parent after its final child

steps.title: directory user-journey acceptance test defense 8 · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 8: Restore an animal-support directory whose image paths, link destinations, alternative text, and card headings fail for changed records and input methods. Defend the repair using a changed animal record, missing-image behavior, meaningful link destinations, keyboard navigation, and a useful assistive outline. Work context: animal support directory incident.
steps.why: Lab: Recover a Broken Animal-Support Directory uses explanation to expose guesses that a passing visual result could hide. This is checkpoint 8 in the animal support directory incident.

content.text: Name the evidence, connect it to the relevant HTML contract, and state what a retest should prove.
